narrative.headline: 2020 Mazda CX-9: engine issues lead a small set of complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 17 complaints with NHTSA, so this is a low-volume picture. The engine is the most reported area at 29% (5 reports), followed by the electrical system at 24% (4 reports). The fuel/propulsion system, service brakes, and forward collision avoidance each draw 2 reports (12%). None of these complaints report a crash, fire, injury, or death.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 5 reports (29%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries noted. The top engine concern to ask about on a test drive.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 4 reports (24%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries noted. Worth a look at the car's electronics.

issues.blurb: 2 reports (12%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries noted. A smaller complaint area.

issues.blurb: 2 reports (12%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries noted. Check the driver-assist features work as expected.
